title: Rocket Lab To Acquire Robotics Leader Motiv Space Systems
text: Rocket Lab Corporation (Nasdaq: RKLB) announced it has signed a definitive agreement to acquire Motiv Space Systems, a California-based robotics and precision mechanisms company. The acquisition is expected to close during Q2 2026. Motiv specializes in robotic arms, actuators, and drive electronics used in ambitious space missions, including NASA's Mars Perseverance rover and CADRE lunar rovers. The acquisition serves two strategic purposes: it adds Mars-proven robotics capabilities for planetary and national security missions, and it vertically integrates spacecraft components like solar array drive assemblies (SADAs) that are often costly and supply-constrained. Founder and CEO Peter Beck stated: Motiv has built a stellar reputation for delivering reliable, high-performance robotics and mechanisms that thrive in the harshest space environments. The acquisition brings Motiv's 50-person engineering team and manufacturing facilities in Pasadena, California into Rocket Lab's ecosystem. Motiv CEO Chris Thayer noted this represents a natural next step allowing them to scale what they have built and support a growing customer base. By integrating these capabilities, Rocket Lab aims to reduce external dependencies, lower costs, accelerate production timelines, and improve quality control for satellite constellation manufacturing.
